title: The week ahead arriving easier than expected
subtitle: A five-minute guided visualisation for seeing the week ahead from the perspective of ease rather than effort.
excerpt: A guided visualisation for Day 17 of the MONEY program. You see the week ahead arriving without the difficulty you have been bracing for — the same tasks and conversations, but lighter.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use on Monday morning or at the start of a week when you are approaching financial tasks with braced-for difficulty.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for Day 17 of the MONEY program: seeing the week ahead from the perspective of ease. The tasks are the same; the expectation is different.
— the grounding principles are the same.
Sit comfortably with your eyes closed. Take three slow breaths.
Picture the week ahead. The specific tasks, conversations, and financial actions that are coming. Let them come into view clearly — not as a calendar but as a sequence of moments.

Now imagine the same week arriving easier than you expected. The difficult conversation that went more smoothly than you thought. The financial task that took less time. The invoice that was paid without a chase. The ask that was received without friction.
Nothing catastrophic has been removed — the week is the same week. The ease is in how it arrives: without the pre-built resistance, without the bracing.
See yourself moving through it. Notice what it feels like to approach tasks from a default of ease rather than a default of difficulty.
Open your eyes when ready. Write one line about what the week looked like from this angle.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
